varieties.name: Sabara
varieties.origin: Minas Gerais & Rio de Janeiro regions, Brazil. Pre-1900s heirloom.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree 4-8m, compact growth. Small to medium fruits 2-3cm, thin dark purple to black skin. Coppery new growth, glossy dark green mature leaves.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet white gelatinous pulp, grape-like texture with tropical flavor notes. Slightly spicy piney skin. 1-3 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive. 3-4 crops per year once mature. Precocious bearing.

varieties.name: Paulista
varieties.origin: São Paulo state, Brazil. Traditional variety, introduced to California 1904, survived until 1917.
varieties.traits: Larger tree 8-12m, vigorous growth. Very large fruits 3-4cm, thick leathery skin. Large lanceolate leaves.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp, slightly tougher skin than Sabara. White to rose-tinted flesh. 1-4 larger seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer. Typically 1-2 major crops per year. Late season.

varieties.name: Red Hybrid (Precocious)
varieties.origin: Brazil hybrid (P. cauliflora x P. aureana). Modern selection for early fruiting.
varieties.traits: Dwarf compact tree 2.5-3m, ideal for containers. Greenish-red to scarlet fruits 2-2.5cm. Dwarfing habit, multi-trunk growth.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, easily peeled thin skin. Balanced sweet-tart flavor profile, similar to blueberry yogurt. Small seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely prolific. 5-7 crops per year in optimal conditions. Back-to-back blooming.

varieties.name: Grimal (Peluda de Alagoas)
varieties.origin: Brazil, introduced to Florida by legendary collector Adolph Grimal. Myrciaria spirito-santensis species.
varieties.traits: Medium upright tree 5-8m. Large purple-black fruits up to 4cm, mildly hairy skin. Upright growth habit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick sweet gelatinous pulp, rich Concord grape flavor with additional complexity. 2-4 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer. 2-3 large crops annually. Begins fruiting 5-7 years.
varieties.availability: Available through specialized Queensland nurseries, sometimes at Daleys.

varieties.name: Giant (Acu Paulista)
varieties.origin: Brazil, Paulista group selection. Selected for exceptional fruit size.
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree 6-10m. Golf ball-sized fruits up to 4cm, largest of all varieties. Weeping graceful branches.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptionally sweet juicy pulp, thick flesh. White gelatinous texture. 2-4 substantial seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, multiple crops per year. Early bearing for size (3-4 years).
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery as 'Giant Jaboticaba', specialized nurseries.

varieties.name: Yellow (Myrciaria glazioviana)
varieties.origin: Brazil. Also called Cabeludinha or Yellow Cabelluda due to fuzzy skin.
varieties.traits: Small bushy tree 3-4m in subtropics. Yellow-green fruits with slight fuzz, 2-2.5cm. Lime-green leaves, powdery appearance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique flavor - concentrated apricot-peach with tangerine notes. Sweet tangy reminiscent of pineapple. Very distinct from black varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer. Fruits in 3 years, faster than black types.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (Kyogle), limited availability Queensland.

varieties.name: Rajada
varieties.origin: Brazil, traditional cultivar.
varieties.traits: Medium tree similar to Paulista. Very large fruits, distinctive green-bronze skin when ripe. Thinner skin than Paulista.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and very good flavor. White pulp, tender texture.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive bearer. Midseason fruiting.
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Australia, rare in Queensland nurseries.

varieties.name: Branca
varieties.origin: Brazil, traditional cultivar. Name means 'white' but fruit is bright green.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree, prolific. Large bright-green fruits (not white). Recommended for home gardens.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Delicious sweet flavor. White pulp. Unusual appearance due to green skin even when ripe.
varieties.yield_notes: Very prolific bearer.

varieties.name: Ponhema
varieties.origin: Brazil, alternative name for M. cauliflora varieties.
varieties.traits: Very large tree, extremely productive. Turnip-shaped large fruits with pointed apex. Somewhat leathery skin.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Must be fully ripe for fresh eating. Often preferred for jelly and preserves due to firmer texture.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely productive, one of highest yielding varieties.
varieties.availability: Limited Australian availability.

varieties.name: Stripey (Poly A)
varieties.origin: Australian selection or Brazilian import. Modern cultivar.
varieties.traits: Distinctive striped fruits - vertical stripe from top to bottom. Medium-sized fruits 2.5-3cm.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet aromatic flavor. Unique appearance makes it highly ornamental.
varieties.yield_notes: Crops heavily within few years of planting. Abundant fruiting against trunk.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (demonstrated in videos), Queensland specialist nurseries.

varieties.name: Trunciflora (Plinia trunciflora)
varieties.origin: Minas Gerais, Brazil. Distinct species, also called Cabinho or small-stemmed jaboticaba.
varieties.traits: Long peduncles with fruits. Globose fruits with 5-20 light green or bronze costate ribs. Large lanceolate dark-green leaves. Fruits often in clusters of up to 6.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet juicy pulp of excellent quality. 2-4 substantial seeds. Red fruit with light green streaks when mature.
varieties.yield_notes: Short juvenile period - fruits at 4-6 years from seed.
varieties.availability: Rare in Australian cultivation, collector's item.

varieties.name: Coroa
varieties.origin: Brazil, sent to USDA in 1908, one of first varieties introduced to USA.
varieties.traits: Large tree, M. cauliflora type. Larger fruits than standard varieties.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp, good quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, historical significance.

varieties.name: Murta
varieties.origin: Brazil, introduced to California 1904, Florida 1911. Survived longest in early USA introductions.
varieties.traits: Smaller leaves than other varieties. Larger fruits. M. cauliflora type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality sweet pulp.
varieties.yield_notes: Historical variety proved successful in Florida until at least 1932.

varieties.name: Roxa (Rujada)
varieties.origin: Brazil, old type. Name means 'red'.
varieties.traits: More reddish than purple fruits, as name implies. Traditional variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red-tinted skin and pulp.
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional bearer.

varieties.name: Mineira
varieties.origin: Brazil, introduced to California 1904. Minas Gerais region variety.
varieties.traits: Traditional M. cauliflora type. Historic variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Standard black fruit, sweet pulp.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer.

varieties.name: IAC 15
varieties.origin: Brazil, Instituto Agronômico de Campinas selection. Modern breeding program.
varieties.traits: Light purple fruits, small to medium 1.5-3cm. Selected for disease resistance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Balanced sweetness with slight acidity. Pleasant flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, disease resistant. Very reliable producer.
varieties.availability: Not widely available Australia, may be found through specialty importers.

varieties.name: Younghan Selection
varieties.origin: Australian or imported selection, documented in Northern NSW trials.
varieties.traits: Reputation as prolific bearer. May be distinct species or cultivar.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality fruits. Size may differ from standard Sabara.
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific bearer, highly productive reputation.
varieties.availability: Limited collector's variety in Australian cultivation.

varieties.name: Whitman
varieties.origin: Imported to Australia, grafted material. Documented in 1980s Australian trials.
varieties.traits: Grafted variety available in Australian collections.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Standard black jaboticaba quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Grafted trees fruit earlier than seedlings.
varieties.availability: Limited Australian collector availability.

varieties.name: Z4 Hybrid
varieties.origin: Modern hybrid selection. Fast-bearing variety.
varieties.traits: Fruits directly on trunk. Hybrid vigor. Compact 3-5m growth.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet flavor, thin skin. Excellent eating quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits frequently throughout year. Productive 3-5 years from planting.

varieties.name: Blue (Myrciaria vexator)
varieties.origin: Costa Rica, Panama, Venezuela. Also called False Jaboticaba or Blue Grape.
varieties.traits: Slow-growing evergreen to 10m. Multiple trunks, peeling bark reveals lighter interior. Lance-shaped glossy green leaves. White snowy flowers with prominent stamen.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bluish-purple edible fruits 2.5-3.8cm, grape-like with fruity aroma. Sweet pulp, pit like grape.
varieties.yield_notes: Greater drought tolerance than true jaboticaba once established.
varieties.availability: Limited specialty sources Australia.

varieties.name: Escarlate (Scarlet)
varieties.origin: Brazil, Red Hybrid type. Selected for vivid color.
varieties.traits: Small compact tree, excellent for containers. Vivid red-scarlet fruits 2-2.5cm. Wide short leaves 6-7cm x 5cm with printed ribs.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, juicy. Superior color, flavor, and precocity to regular red types.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits several times yearly from 3 years age. Fast fruiting.
varieties.availability: Limited Australian availability, may require import.

varieties.name: Small Leaf (Standard Black)
varieties.origin: Brazil, standard M. cauliflora type widely cultivated.
varieties.traits: Compact leaves 2-6cm, standard growth. Black thin-skinned fruits. Slower growing than large leaf.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet aromatic flavor like aromatic lychee. Thin but tough skin. Very good quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Several crops spring to autumn. Takes 5-8 years from seed.

varieties.name: Large Leaf (M. jaboticaba)
varieties.origin: Brazil, M. jaboticaba species. Also called São Paulo type.
varieties.traits: Much faster grower than small leaf. Larger leaves. Larger fruits than small leaf, slightly tougher skin. Only 1-2 crops per year.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good sweet flavor, slightly less refined than small leaf. Larger size appealing.
varieties.yield_notes: Fewer crops annually but faster tree growth. Good for quick canopy.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, some Queensland suppliers.

varieties.name: Sabara
varieties.origin: Minas Gerais & Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. Most popular variety worldwide.
varieties.traits: Medium tree 4-8m, compact. Small-medium fruits 2-3cm, thin black-purple skin. Glossy foliage.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet white gelatinous pulp, grape-like tropical flavor. 1-3 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: 3-4 crops annually with continuous irrigation. Precocious bearing.
varieties.availability: Greens of tropical monsoon climates (Kottayam), Veliyath Gardens (Aluva), Cheerakuzhy Nursery (Palakkad), widely available tropical monsoon climates nurseries.

varieties.name: Red Hybrid (Precocious)
varieties.origin: Brazil hybrid (P. cauliflora x P. aureana).
varieties.traits: Dwarf 2.5-3m, perfect containers. Greenish-red scarlet fruits 2-2.5cm. Multi-trunk growth, dwarfing habit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, thin easily-peeled skin. Balanced sweet-tart. Small seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely prolific, 5-7 crops annually. Back-to-back blooming.

varieties.name: Escarlate (Scarlet)
varieties.origin: Brazil, Red Hybrid selection.
varieties.traits: Small container-friendly tree. Vivid red-scarlet fruits 2-2.5cm. Wide ribbed leaves 6-7cm x 5cm.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet juicy. Superior to regular red in color, flavor, precocity.
varieties.yield_notes: Multiple crops yearly from 3 years. Fast fruiting.
varieties.availability: Greens of tropical monsoon climates (layering plants), ships tropical monsoon climates/Tamil Nadu/Karnataka.

varieties.name: Yellow (Myrciaria glazioviana)
varieties.origin: Brazil. Cabeludinha or Cabelluda (fuzzy).
varieties.traits: Small bushy 3-4m. Yellow-green fuzzy fruits 2-2.5cm. Lime-green powdery leaves.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique apricot-peach-tangerine flavor. Sweet tangy pineapple notes. Very distinct.
varieties.yield_notes: Faster fruiting - 3 years. Good producer.
varieties.availability: Limited tropical monsoon climates availability, may be available through Greens of tropical monsoon climates or specialty imports.

varieties.name: Blue (Myrciaria vexator)
varieties.origin: Costa Rica, Panama, Venezuela. False Jaboticaba.
varieties.traits: Evergreen to 10m. Multiple trunks, peeling bark. Lance-shaped glossy leaves. White showy flowers.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bluish-purple fruits 2.5-3.8cm. Grape-like sweet pulp with fruity aroma.
varieties.yield_notes: Greater drought tolerance. Ornamental landscape value.
varieties.availability: Greens of tropical monsoon climates (layer plants), Veliyath Gardens (Aluva).

varieties.name: Oblongata (Myrciaria oblongata)
varieties.origin: Brazil. Also called Jabuticaba Azeda (sour jaboticaba).
varieties.traits: Multiple trunks, peeling bark. Lance-shaped glossy bright green leaves. White prominent stamen flowers.
varieties.flesh_fruit: More acidic than standard types. Sour notes. Used fresh or processing.
varieties.yield_notes: Tolerates variety of soils. Good drought tolerance once established.

varieties.name: Grimal (Peluda de Alagoas)
varieties.origin: Brazil, Myrciaria spirito-santensis. Named for collector Adolph Grimal.
varieties.traits: Medium upright tree 5-8m. Large purple-black fruits to 4cm, mildly hairy skin.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick sweet gelatinous pulp. Rich Concord grape flavor with complexity. 2-4 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer, 2-3 large crops annually. Fruits 5-7 years.
varieties.availability: Veliyath Gardens (Aluva), may be available Greens of tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Paulista
varieties.origin: São Paulo state, Brazil. Traditional cultivar.
varieties.traits: Large tree 8-12m, vigorous. Very large fruits 3-4cm, thick leathery skin. Large leaves.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp, tougher skin. White to rose-tinted flesh. 1-4 larger seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, 1-2 major crops. Late season. Rust-resistant.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty tropical monsoon climates nurseries or Greens of tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Acu Paulista (Giant)
varieties.origin: Brazil, Paulista group. Selected for exceptional size.
varieties.traits: Medium-large 6-10m. Golf ball-sized fruits to 4cm. Weeping branches.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptionally sweet juicy, thick flesh. White gelatinous. 2-4 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy, multiple crops yearly. Early bearing 3-4 years.
varieties.availability: Veliyath Gardens (Aluva), specialized tropical monsoon climates nurseries.

varieties.name: Precoce (Dwarf Red)
varieties.origin: Brazil, rare dwarf red variety.
varieties.traits: Rare dwarf, container-perfect. Fruits when 2-3 feet tall. Compact growth ideal small spaces.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red fruits, sweet. Early bearing benefits.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits 2-3 years from seed. Multiple annual harvests.
varieties.availability: Veliyath Gardens (rare variety), may require special order.

varieties.name: Trunciflora (Plinia trunciflora)
varieties.origin: Minas Gerais, Brazil. Cabinho (small-stemmed).
varieties.traits: Long peduncles. Globose fruits with 5-20 light green/bronze costate ribs. Large lanceolate dark leaves. Clusters of up to 6 fruits.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet juicy excellent quality. Red fruit with light green streaks. 2-4 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Short juvenile period - fruits 4-6 years from seed.
varieties.availability: Veliyath Gardens (Aluva), rare in tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Coronata (Myrciaria coronata)
varieties.origin: Brazil. Jabuticaba Coroada or King Jaboticaba.
varieties.traits: Distinct species. Variation in fruit characteristics from standard cauliflora.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Some types incredible quality, others less so. Worth trying different selections.
varieties.yield_notes: Variable depending on selection.

varieties.name: Standard Black (Small Leaf)
varieties.origin: Brazil, M. cauliflora standard type.
varieties.traits: Compact leaves 2-6cm. Black thin-skinned fruits. Standard growth.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet aromatic, very good quality. Thin tough skin.
varieties.yield_notes: Several crops with continuous irrigation. Year-round in tropics.

varieties.name: Rajada
varieties.origin: Brazil, traditional cultivar.
varieties.traits: Medium tree. Very large fruits, green-bronze skin distinctive. Thinner skin than Paulista.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet very good flavor. White pulp, tender.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, midseason.

varieties.name: Ponhema
varieties.origin: Brazil, M. cauliflora type.
varieties.traits: Very large tree, extremely productive. Turnip-shaped large fruits, pointed apex. Leathery skin.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Must be fully ripe for fresh eating. Preferred for jelly/preserves.
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely productive, highest yielding.
varieties.availability: May be available specialized tropical monsoon climates nurseries.

varieties.name: Branca
varieties.origin: Brazil. Name 'white' but fruit bright green.
varieties.traits: Medium prolific tree. Large bright-green fruits (not white).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Delicious sweet. White pulp. Green even when ripe.
varieties.yield_notes: Very prolific.

varieties.name: IAC 15
varieties.origin: Brazil, Instituto Agronômico de Campinas. Modern breeding.
varieties.traits: Light purple fruits 1.5-3cm. Disease resistant selection.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Balanced sweet with slight acid. Pleasant profile.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, disease resistant. Very reliable.
varieties.availability: Not widely available tropical monsoon climates, may require specialty import.

varieties.name: Fukuoka
varieties.origin: Selection grown in southern China, introduced Taiwan 2004.
varieties.traits: Large size, juicy flesh. Low acid-sugar ratio. Less volatile compounds.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Juicy, large fruits. Milder flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer. Suitable for juice processing.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty tropical monsoon climates importers with Asian connections.

varieties.name: Argentina
varieties.origin: Selection grown in southern China alongside Fukuoka.
varieties.traits: Rich in volatile compounds when ripe. Slightly lighter taste.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good sugar content, abundant volatiles. Complex aroma.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer. Suitable for dry products.
varieties.availability: Specialty import possibility tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Roxa (Rujada)
varieties.origin: Brazil, old type. 'Red' variety.
varieties.traits: More reddish than purple. Traditional.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red-tinted skin and pulp.
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional bearer.

varieties.name: Black Hybrid
varieties.origin: Plinia cauliflora x aureana cross. Modern hybrid.
varieties.traits: Compact dwarfing habit, container-ideal. Precocious fruiting 3-4 years optimal, 6-8 temperate.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Ultra-sweet flavorful. Thin easily-peeling skin. Better taste than classic blacks.
varieties.yield_notes: Early fruiting, compact size. Multiple crops.
varieties.availability: May be available specialty tropical monsoon climates nurseries or import.

varieties.name: Grandifolia (Myrciaria grandifolia)
varieties.origin: Brazil. Jabuticaba Graúda or Large Jaboticaba.
varieties.traits: Larger tree, larger leaves than standard. Larger fruits.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, larger size.
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer of large fruits.
